zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry Carter於1540年出生在英國素可霍沙安時，他的父親John Carter36歲，母親Mary Ann Thorndick40歲。他於1561年6月15日與Jone Pilcher在英國英格蘭素可霍沙安結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1567年11月23日在英國英格蘭素福爾可享年27歲。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry Carter was born in 1540, in Horsham, Sussex, England, his father, John Carter, was 36 and his mother, Mary Ann Thorndick, was 40. He married Jone Pilcher on 15 June 1561, in Horsham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 23 November 1567, in Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 27.

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Carter(이)가 1540년 영국 수세스 호르샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Carter(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Ann Thorndick(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1561년 6월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 호르샤에서 Jone Pilcher(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1567년 11월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry Carter родился в 1540 г. в местности хауршам, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени John Carter было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Ann Thorndick, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Jone Pilcher 15 июня 1561 г. в местности хауршам, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 23 ноября 1567 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 27 лет.
